columns.extraction_plan: Extract the alphanumeric code from the main title on page 1, or from the "ETHICS STATEMENT" section on page 12.
The PubMed ID is not reported in the PDF. The document provides a DOI (10.1002/ijc.34018), but not a PubMed ID.
Extract the full title of the study from the top of page 1.
Extract the first author's name and use "et al." for the rest, as there is a long list of authors on page 1.
Extract the "Accepted" date year from the top left of page 1, or the publication year from the "How to cite this article" section on page 13.
Based on the comprehensive nature of the document (multiple pages, detailed methods, results, discussion, figures, and tables), it is a full publication.
Locate the "KEYWORDS" section on page 3 and extract the phase mentioned. Also, reference 15 on page 13 explicitly states "phase 3 trial".
Extract the phrase "5-year follow-up results" from the main title on page 1, or "long-term analysis" from the abstract on page 2, or "long-term results" from the "What's new?" section on page 3.
